journal_entry.reason: Atlas Van Lines borrowed $200,000 from its bank. Under IFRS, the initial recognition of a financial liability is at fair value, which equals the cash received. The debit to Cash reflects the asset increase of $200,000. The credit to Loan Payable reflects the liability increase of $200,000. Interest will be recognized as expense in future periods as it accrues; no interest is recorded at the inception of the loan. The entry balances with total debits of $200,000 and total credits of $200,000. No tax applies to loan proceeds.

resolved_ambiguities.resolution: Transaction explicitly states 'borrowed $200,000 in cash' — cash received is clear
Explicitly stated: 6% annual rate, payable semi-annually
Not needed — loan proceeds received and liability recorded at same amount regardless of when transaction occurred

output.asset_increase_reason: Cash increases by $200,000 when the loan proceeds are received from the bank.
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: No expenses are incurred at the time of borrowing. Interest expense will be recorded later when it accrues or is paid, not at loan inception.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid down or decreased. The loan payable is being created (credit side), not reduced.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are affected by this borrowing transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue accounts are affected by this borrowing transaction.

output.liability_increase_reason: The bank loan of $200,000 creates a notes payable or loan payable liability that increases.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts are affected; this is a borrowing transaction, not an owner investment.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is earned; borrowing cash creates a liability, not income.
output.asset_decrease_reason: No assets decrease on the credit side; cash increases (debit side) from receiving the loan proceeds.
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No dividends or owner drawings are involved in this borrowing transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are decreased; the interest expense will be recognized in future periods when incurred, not at the time of borrowing.

incomplete_information_checks.reason: All necessary information is present: principal amount ($200,000), interest rate (6%), term (3 years), and payment frequency (semi-annually). This is a straightforward loan receipt transaction.

debit_asset_increase.reason: Accept. The company receives $200,000 cash from the bank, which increases the Cash asset account.

debit_dividend_increase.reason: No dividend transaction in this borrowing event.

debit_expense_increase.reason: No expense is recognized at the time of borrowing. Interest expense will be recognized in future periods as it accrues.

debit_liability_decrease.reason: This is a new borrowing, not a repayment. No liability is decreased.

debit_equity_decrease.reason: Borrowing does not affect equity accounts.

debit_revenue_decrease.reason: Borrowing does not affect revenue accounts.

credit_liability_increase.reason: Accept. The company incurs a $200,000 obligation to the bank, which increases the Loan Payable liability account.

credit_equity_increase.reason: Borrowing does not affect equity accounts.

credit_asset_decrease.reason: No asset is decreased in this borrowing transaction. Cash is received, not paid out.

credit_dividend_decrease.reason: No dividend transaction in this borrowing event.

credit_expense_decrease.reason: No expense is decreased in this borrowing transaction.

entry.reason: Atlas Van Lines borrowed $200,000 from its bank. Under IFRS, the initial recognition of a financial liability is at fair value, which equals the cash received. The debit to Cash reflects the asset increase of $200,000. The credit to Loan Payable reflects the liability increase of $200,000. Interest will be recognized as expense in future periods as it accrues; no interest is recorded at the inception of the loan. The entry balances with total debits of $200,000 and total credits of $200,000. No tax applies to loan proceeds.

output.decision_rationale: This is a straightforward loan receipt transaction. All necessary information is present: the principal amount ($200,000), the parties involved (Atlas Van Lines and its bank), and the loan terms (6% annual interest, 3-year term, semi-annual payments). The classifier tuples correctly identify this as a debit to an asset (Cash) and a credit to a liability (Loan Payable). Under IFRS 9, financial liabilities are initially recognized at fair value, which in this case equals the cash proceeds received. Interest expense will be recognized in future periods using the effective interest method as interest accrues. The entry is balanced (debits = credits = $200,000), uses appropriate account names, and follows IFRS standards. No tax applies to loan proceeds.

Explicitly stated: 6% annual rate, payable semi-annually
Not needed — loan proceeds received and liability recorded at same amount regardless of when transaction occurred
